revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[東京美術]] to [[Tokyo Bijutsu]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{一次資料|date=2022年12月9日 (金) 10:31 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 会社 |社名 = 株式会社東京美術 |英文社名 = TOKYO BIJUTSU Co.,Ltd. |ロゴ = |画像 = |画像説明 = |種類 = [[株式会社 (日本)|株式会社]] |市場情報 = 非上場 |略称 = |国籍 = {{JPN}} |本社郵便番号 = 170-0011 |本社所在地 = [[東京都]][[豊島区]]池袋本町3-31-15 |本店郵便番号 = |本店所在地 = |設立 = [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年） |業種 = 5250 |統一金融機関コード = |SWIFTコード = |事業内容 = 図書出版及びマーチャンダイジング業務 他 |代表者 = [[代表取締役]][[社長]] 永澤順司 |資本金 = 3,500万円 |発行済株式総数 = |売上高 = |営業利益 = |経常利益 = |純利益 = * 1億8,439万1,000円 （2025年3月期）<ref name="fy2025">[https://catr.jp/companies/43cbb/21456/settlements/82bba/416847 株式会社東京美術 第66期決算公告]</ref> |純資産 = |総資産 = * 45億1,637万1,000円 （2025年3月期）<ref name="fy2025" /> |従業員数 = 193名（2021年5月31日現在） |支店舗数 = |決算期 = 3月31日 |主要株主 = [[シチズン時計]]株式会社 71.9% |主要子会社 = |関係する人物 = |外部リンク = https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ |特記事項 = }} '''株式会社東京美術'''（とうきょうびじゅつ）は、[[東京都]][[豊島区]]にある[[日本]]の[[出版社]]。[[シチズン時計]]グループの一員。
== 概要 == [[1960年]]（昭和35年）に創立された出版社。社章は英国の著名な陶芸家[[バーナード・リーチ]]の作品で「リーチマーク」と呼称している。
映像部門があり、[[テレビ朝日]]と[[テレビ東京]]の報道・情報番組用途の生放送[[テロップ]]制作なども受託している。
== 沿革 == * [[1960年]]（昭和35年）3月 - 宣伝企画・出版・印刷業として会社設立 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年） ** 9月 - [[東京国立博物館]]の出版・印刷業務取引開始。シチズン時計の印刷および販売促進業務の取引開始 ** 10月 - NET（現：テレビ朝日）テロップ業務開始 * [[1963年]]（昭和38年）5月 - オフセット印刷部門新設 * [[1965年]]（昭和40年）8月 - オフセット製版部門新設 * [[1976年]]（昭和51年）4月 - オフセット製版部門を分離、株式会社 東京プロセスを設立 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）12月 - 主要取引先300社を達成 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）3月 - 組版部門を分離し、株式会社 東京美術製版を設立 * [[1990年]]（平成2年）4月 - 株式会社 東京美術製版を株式会社 アートワード東京と社名変更 * [[1991年]]（平成3年）3月 - シチズン商事の子会社となる * [[1992年]]（平成4年）3月 - オフセット印刷部門を分離し、株式会社 東美制作センターを設立 * [[1997年]]（平成9年）11月 - 電子作画室研修室を設立 * [[2000年]]（平成12年） ** 2月 - 株式会社 東美制作センターを吸収、印刷事業部オフセット部に変更 ** 6月 - 香港を拠点とした中国での印刷開始 * [[2003年]]（平成15年）9月 - 香港オフィス開設 * [[2004年]]（平成16年） ** 4月 - シチズン時計株式会社の子会社となる ** 4月 - 開発部を新設 同年9月映像センターをリニューアル * [[2005年]]（平成17年）1月 - CSR委員会設立 同年5月本社ビルをリニューアル * [[2007年]]（平成19年）5月 - プライバシーマーク使用許可を取得 * [[2010年]]（平成22年）3月 - 創立50周年。
== 主な出版物 == * すぐわかるシリーズ ** 「東京美術」すぐわかるシリーズ
* もっと知りたいシリーズ
* 考古学シリーズ ** 先土器時代の知識(No.3)、[[鈴木忠司]] 著 ** 古墳時代の知識(No.6)、[[岩崎卓也]] 著 ** 貝塚の獣骨の知識(No.10)、[[金子浩昌]] 著 ** 装身具と骨角製漁具の知識(No.13)、[[江坂輝彌]]／[[渡辺誠]] 共著 ** 先史時代の自然環境 -縄文時代の自然史(No.21)、松島義章／前田保夫 著 ** 考古学の知識(No.1) ** 発掘と整理の知識(No.2) ** 縄文時代の知識(No.4) ** 弥生時代の知識(No.5) ** 歴史時代の知識(No.7) ** 地層の知識 -第四紀をさぐる(No.8)、町田洋／新井房夫／森脇広 著 ** 化石の知識(No.9)、江坂輝彌 著 ** 旧石器の知識(No.11) ** 新石器の知識(No.12) ** 縄文土器の知識Ⅰ(No.14) ** 縄文土器の知識Ⅱ(No.15) ** 弥生土器の知識(No.16) ** 土師器・須恵器の知識(No.17) ** 土偶の知識(No.18) ** 古墳の知識Ⅰ(No.19) ** 古墳の知識Ⅱ(No.20) ** 原始・歴史時代の歴史環境(No.22)
* 基礎の考古学シリーズ ** 土師器・須恵器の知識（改訂新版）、玉口時雄／小金井靖 共著 ** 発掘と整理の知識（改訂新版）、服部敬史 著 ** 縄文土器の知識 1―草創・早・前期―（改訂新版）、麻生優・白石浩之 著 ** 縄文土器の知識 2―中・後・晩期―（改訂新版）、藤村東男 著 ** 地層の知識 ―第四紀をさぐる（改訂新版）、町田洋／新井房夫／森脇広 共著
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の出版社一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ 株式会社東京美術公式サイト] {{Normdaten}} {{Company-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とうきようひしゆつ}} [[Category:日本の出版社]] [[Category:美術に関する出版社]] [[Category:豊島区の企業]] [[Category:1960年設立の企業]] [[Category:販売支援業・販売促進業]] [[Category:シチズンホールディングス]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tokyo Bijutsu Co., Ltd.''' (株式会社東京美術) is a Japanese publishing company based in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] and part of the [[:en:Citizen Watch|Citizen Watch Group]].
== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].
The company also operates a media division, handling live broadcast subtitle production for news and information programs on [[:en:TV Asahi|TV Asahi]] and [[:en:TV Tokyo|TV Tokyo]].
== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|qq=|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|qq=|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tokyo Bijutsu Co., Ltd.''' (株式会社東京美術) is a Japanese publishing company based in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] and part of the [[:en:Citizen Watch|Citizen Watch Group]].
== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].
The company also operates a media division, handling live broadcast subtitle production for news and information programs on [[:en:TV Asahi|TV Asahi]] and [[:en:TV Tokyo|TV Tokyo]].
== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|qq=|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].

== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|qq=|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].

== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

(株式会社東京美術) is a Japanese publishing company based in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] and part of the [[:en:Citizen Watch|Citizen Watch Group]].
== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].
The company also operates a media division, handling live broadcast subtitle production for news and information programs on [[:en:TV Asahi|TV Asahi]] and [[:en:TV Tokyo|TV Tokyo]].
== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東京美術]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東京美術]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東京美術]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tokyo Bijutsu Co., Ltd.''' (株式会社東京美術) is a Japanese publishing company based in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] and part of the [[:en:Citizen Watch|Citizen Watch Group]].
== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].
The company also operates a media division, handling live broadcast subtitle production for news and information programs on [[:en:TV Asahi|TV Asahi]] and [[:en:TV Tokyo|TV Tokyo]].
== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東京美術]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東京美術]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東京美術]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tokyo Bijutsu Co., Ltd.''' (株式会社東京美術) is a Japanese publishing company based in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] and part of the [[:en:Citizen Watch|Citizen Watch Group]].
== Overview == Established in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist [[:en:Bernard Leach|Bernard Leach]].
The company also operates a media division, handling live broadcast subtitle production for news and information programs on [[:en:TV Asahi|TV Asahi]] and [[:en:TV Tokyo|TV Tokyo]].
== History == * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * [[:en:1961|1961]] (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * [[:en:1963|1963]] (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * [[:en:1965|1965]] (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * [[:en:1976|1976]] (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1980|1980]] (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * [[:en:1987|1987]] (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * [[:en:1991|1991]] (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * [[:en:1997|1997]] (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * [[:en:2003|2003]] (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * [[:en:2004|2004]] (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * [[:en:2010|2010]] (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

(株式会社東京美術) is a Japanese publishing company based in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} and part of the {{ill|Citizen Watch|en|Citizen Watch|WD=Q1368019|lt=Citizen Watch Group}}.
== Overview == Established in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Showa 35), Tokyo Bijutsu is a publisher with a focus on art and archaeology. Its corporate logo, known as the "Leach Mark," was designed by renowned British ceramic artist {{ill|Bernard Leach|en|Bernard Leach|WD=Q2898210|lt=Bernard Leach}}.
The company also operates a media division, handling live broadcast subtitle production for news and information programs on {{ill|TV Asahi|en|TV Asahi|WD=Q908436|lt=TV Asahi}} and {{ill|TV Tokyo|en|TV Tokyo|WD=Q734663|lt=TV Tokyo}}.
== History == * {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Showa 35), March: Founded as a company for advertising, publishing, and printing. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|WD=Q3696|lt=1961}} (Showa 36): ** September: Began publishing and printing work for {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}; started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * {{ill|1963|en|1963|WD=Q2755|lt=1963}} (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}} (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1980|en|1980|WD=Q2439|lt=1980}} (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}} (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990}} (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}} (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}} (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
== Financials (2024) == * Capital: ¥35 million * Net Income: ¥160,115,000 * Total Assets: ¥4,389,385,000 * Employees: 193 (as of May 31, 2021) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Publishing Companies|ja|日本の出版社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Publishing Companies|1=List of Japanese Publishing Companies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505792}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/ Official Website] {{Company-stub}}

started printing and sales promotion services for Citizen Watch. ** October: Began subtitling operations for NET (now TV Asahi). * {{ill|1963|en|1963|WD=Q2755|lt=1963}} (Showa 38), May: Established offset printing division. * {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}} (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1980|en|1980|WD=Q2439|lt=1980}} (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}} (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990}} (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}} (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}} (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.

division. * {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} (Showa 40), August: Established offset platemaking division. * {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Showa 51), April: Spun off the offset platemaking division to form Tokyo Process Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1980|en|1980|WD=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}} (Showa 55), December: Reached 300 major clients. * {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} (Showa 62), March: Spun off the typesetting division to form Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}} (Heisei 2), April: Renamed Tokyo Bijutsu Typesetting Co., Ltd. to Artword Tokyo. * {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} (Heisei 3), March: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Shoji. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4), March: Spun off the offset printing division to establish Tobisei Center Co., Ltd. * {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} (Heisei 9), November: Established digital animation training center. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}} (Heisei 12): ** February: Absorbed Tobisei Center Co., Ltd., renaming the division Offset Department of the Printing Division. ** June: Began printing operations in China, based in Hong Kong. * {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}} (Heisei 15), September: Opened Hong Kong office. * {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}} (Heisei 16): ** April: Became a subsidiary of Citizen Watch Co., Ltd. ** September: Renewed video production center. * {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17): ** January: Established CSR committee. ** May: Renovated headquarters building. * {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}} (Heisei 19), May: Acquired Privacy Mark certification. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}} (Heisei 22), March: Celebrated its 50th anniversary.
== Key Publications == * "Easy to Understand" Series ** [http://www.tokyo-bijutsu.co.jp/catalog/series/index.html View Tokyo Bijutsu's Easy to Understand Series] * "Want to Know More" Series * Archaeology Series ** Examples include works on prehistoric, Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods, as well as environmental studies and artifact analysis.
